creator: john.hein@microsemi.com
text:
This can still fail if you select GSSAPI_BASE and have security/krb5
installed (possibly for some other port).  See updated how-to-repeat
instructions at the end of this email.

Please re-open this PR since the failure case is still the same.

To fix this, you need to make sure -L/usr/lib is in your LDFLAGS
before any -L/usr/local/lib.

Just adding GSSAPI_BASE_LDFLAGS=-L/usr/lib may not work because you
can't control the evaluation order of <OPT>_LDFLAGS (and so
-L/usr/local/lib added if, for instance, ldap is turned on might occur
before -L/usr/lib).

The following patch to Makefile works to add -L/usr/lib at the front
of LDFLAGS.

Index: Makefile
===================================================================
--- Makefile	(revision 349198)
+++ Makefile	(working copy)
@@ -128,6 +128,12 @@
 
 .include <bsd.port.options.mk>
 
+.if ${PORT_OPTIONS:MGSSAPI_BASE}
+# try to make sure -L/usr/lib is first, else you may try to link with the
+# wrong kerberos libraries
+LDFLAGS=	-L/usr/lib
+.endif
+
 .if ${PORT_OPTIONS:MCA_BUNDLE} && !${PORT_OPTIONS:MOPENSSL}
 IGNORE=		only supports CA bundle with OpenSSL
 .endif



But if you turn on the CARES option, that is not enough because the
configure script inserts -L/usr/local/lib before LDFLAGS when it tries
to test for the c-ares version.


That's why the original patch includes the patch for configure.
So, you need to apply that, too.

And that's why the 'how-to-repeat' instructions say to build with LDAP
& CARES turned on.  Now that the port has changed a little, the
failure is still the same, but the how-to-repeat instructions should
be updated slightly to match the new option name:

how-to-repeat is now:

install security/krb5
build ftp/curl with GSSAPI_BASE & LDAP & CARES turned on.
